title: Add optional CF Access config + a JWKS-verified JWT module (no wiring yet)
action: Add optional, non-secret Cloudflare Access config fields cf_access_team_domain: Option<String> and cf_access_aud: Option<String> to the config struct in src/config/mod.rs (the JSON-file-backed Config/RawConfig, not the env-only Secrets struct -- neither value is a secret: the team domain and Application Audience tag are both fine in the JSON config file). Both fields must default to None so a config file that omits them loads exactly as before (deny_unknown_fields elsewhere in this struct means you must add the fields with #[serde(default)]). Then add a new module src/web/cf_access.rs implementing Cloudflare Access JWT verification: (1) fetch and cache the JWKS from {team_domain}/cdn-cgi/access/certs using the existing reqwest client (look at how reqwest is already used elsewhere in the repo for the client-construction pattern), with an in-memory cache keyed by kid and a bounded TTL so it isn't refetched on every request; (2) a function that takes the raw Cf-Access-Jwt-Assertion header value plus the configured team_domain/aud and returns Ok(<email or subject claim>) only if the JWT's RS256 signature verifies against the cached JWKS AND iss equals the team domain AND aud contains the configured aud tag AND exp/nbf are valid -- any single failure must be Err, never a partial pass. Add the jsonwebtoken crate (a current 9.x version) to Cargo.toml dependencies for this. Wire the new module's mod cf_access; declaration into src/web/mod.rs but do NOT call it from auth.rs yet -- that is the next cell. This cell is pure addition: it must not change the behavior of any existing endpoint, and building with no cf_access_* config set must be identical to today.

truths: cf_access_team_domain and cf_access_aud are optional (Option<String>, default None) on the JSON-file config, not on the env-only Secrets struct
A config file that omits these two keys still loads successfully (existing deny_unknown_fields / required-field behavior for every other field is untouched)

src/web/cf_access.rs verifies RS256 signature via a JWKS fetched from {team_domain}/cdn-cgi/access/certs, and separately checks iss == team_domain, aud contains the configured aud, and exp/nbf validity -- verification must fail closed (Err) on any single mismatch, never trust the header without a full signature check
The JWKS is cached (keyed by kid, bounded TTL), not refetched on every call
No existing endpoint's behavior changes when cf_access_team_domain/cf_access_aud are unset -- cf_access.rs is not called from any request path yet

artifacts.substantive: JWKS fetch+cache and CF Access JWT verification function(s), unit-testable without a live network call (structure the signature/claims verification so it can be tested against a locally-generated RSA keypair + hand-built JWT, not requiring a real Cloudflare endpoint)
cf_access_team_domain / cf_access_aud optional fields added with serde defaults

prohibitions: Do not touch src/web/auth.rs in this cell -- wiring is the next cell
Do not remove or weaken the existing HERDR_GO_WEB_SECRET / AuthSession token flow
Do not make cf_access.rs trust the Cf-Access-Jwt-Assertion header's claims without a full JWKS signature check

verify: cargo build --quiet 2>&1 | tail -50 && cargo test --quiet cf_access 2>&1 | tail -50

trace.outcome: Added optional non-secret cf_access_team_domain/cf_access_aud config fields and a new src/web/cf_access.rs (JWKS fetch+cache by kid with 1h TTL, fail-closed RS256+iss+aud+exp/nbf verification), wired mod cf_access; no auth.rs change, no behavior change when unset.
trace.verify_output: build: clean (no errors). cf_access tests: running 11 tests ........... test result: ok. 11 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored; 251 filtered out. Full suite also green: 262 passed / 0 failed (no regressions).
